title: extra_css injected raw into HTML templates enables style injection
description:
server.rs index() and serve_profile_html() interpolate state.extra_css directly into a <style> tag via format!():
  <style>{extra_css}</style>
The extra_css value comes from theme_tokens_path (parsed JSON values) and custom_css_path (raw file contents). Neither is sanitized for HTML special characters. A custom CSS file containing '</style><script>alert(1)</script><style>' breaks out of the style block and injects arbitrary HTML/JS. While the config is operator-controlled today, this is a foot-gun for anyone adding user-facing theme customization, and the CSP header uses 'unsafe-inline' for styles which would not block it. Fix: HTML-escape the CSS string, or load it from a separate CSS file endpoint with proper Content-Type.
close_reason: extra_css now strips </style> to prevent style tag breakout
